title: Where should you start?
intro: This short check finds the right starting point in the French course by asking a few questions that get harder as you go. It takes about two minutes, and you can retake it any time.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Je ___ Marie.' (I am Marie)
questions.explanation: With je, the verb être takes the form suis.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'good evening'?
questions.explanation: Bonsoir greets someone in the evening, while bonne nuit is said only when leaving to sleep.

questions.prompt: You are introduced to someone for the first time. What do you say?
questions.explanation: Enchanté is the standard reply when meeting someone for the first time.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Nous ___ deux enfants.' (we have)
questions.explanation: The verb avoir with nous is avons.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ table est grande.'
questions.explanation: Table is feminine and singular, so it takes la.

questions.prompt: Which number is 'quatre-vingts'?
questions.explanation: Quatre-vingts means four twenties, so 80.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Nous ___ au cinéma ce soir.' (we are going)
questions.explanation: The verb aller with nous is allons.

questions.prompt: Complete the negative: 'Je n'ai ___ voiture.' (I do not have a car)
questions.explanation: After a negation, the indefinite article becomes de.

questions.prompt: How do you say the time 14:30 in French?
questions.explanation: The 24 hour clock gives quatorze heures for 14, plus trente for the minutes.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Elle ___ allée à Paris.' (she went)
questions.explanation: Aller forms its passé composé with être, and the participle agrees: elle est allée.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Avant, nous ___ à la campagne.' (we used to live)
questions.explanation: A repeated or ongoing past situation takes the imparfait.

questions.prompt: In a bakery, which is the polite way to ask for a baguette?
options: Je voudrais une baguette, s'il vous plaît.
J'ai une baguette, s'il vous plaît.
questions.answer: Je voudrais une baguette, s'il vous plaît.
questions.explanation: Je voudrais softens the request, while je veux sounds blunt in a shop.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Tu as téléphoné à ton frère ? Oui, je ___ ai téléphoné.'
questions.explanation: Téléphoner à takes an indirect object, so a single person is lui.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Tu veux du pain ? Oui, j'___ veux.'
questions.explanation: En replaces a quantity introduced by du, de la or des.

questions.prompt: Complete the futur simple: 'L'année prochaine, nous ___ en Espagne.' (we will go)
questions.explanation: The futur simple of aller with nous is irons.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ce livre est ___ intéressant que l'autre.' (less interesting)
questions.explanation: The comparative of inferiority is moins plus adjective plus que.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'La femme ___ parle est ma voisine.'
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun is the subject of parle, so it is qui.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'J'ai décidé ___ partir seul.'
questions.explanation: Décider is followed by de before an infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Je ne pense pas qu'il ___ raison.'
questions.explanation: Penser in the negative expresses doubt and triggers the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Si nous ___ plus de temps, nous resterions une semaine.'
questions.explanation: With a conditionnel in the main clause, si takes the imparfait.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Espérer takes the indicative, unlike vouloir and il faut which take the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Le jour ___ je suis arrivé, il pleuvait.'
questions.explanation: After an expression of time such as le jour, the relative pronoun is où.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quand je suis arrivé, il ___ déjà parti.'
questions.explanation: Partir uses être, so the plus que parfait is il était parti.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Il travaille ___ écoutant de la musique.'
questions.explanation: The gérondif is formed with en plus the present participle.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Je te préviens ___ tu saches la vérité.' (so that you know)
questions.explanation: A purpose clause with its own subject needs pour que plus the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete the passive: 'Le pont ___ en 1889 par un architecte italien.'
questions.explanation: A completed past passive event uses the passé composé of être plus the participle.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Si tu m'avais prévenu, je ___ t'aider.' (I could have helped you)
questions.explanation: An unreal past hypothesis pairs the plus que parfait with the conditionnel passé.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quoiqu'il ___ de son mieux, il échoue toujours.'
questions.explanation: Quoique introduces a concession and is always followed by the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Je suis content que tu ___ venu hier.'
questions.explanation: The past subjunctive uses the subjunctive of the auxiliary, here que tu sois venu.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Il faudrait que nous ___ avant midi.'
questions.explanation: Il faut que keeps the subjunctive even when the main verb is in the conditionnel.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ me dérange, c'est le bruit.'
questions.explanation: In this emphatic structure the pronoun is the subject of dérange, so it is ce qui.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Il n'avait presque pas révisé ; ___, il a réussi l'examen.'
questions.explanation: The second clause contradicts what the first leads you to expect, which calls for a concessive connector.

questions.prompt: Which is the most formal way to say 'I don't know'?
questions.explanation: Ignorer belongs to formal register, while the others are spoken or elliptical.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Dans cette affaire, il s'agit ___ un simple malentendu.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal turn is il s'agit de, and de elides before a vowel.

questions.prompt: Which noun corresponds to the verb 'découvrir'?
questions.explanation: La découverte is the derived noun, while le découvert means a bank overdraft.

questions.prompt: What does the idiom 'poser un lapin à quelqu'un' mean?
options: To fail to show up for a meeting
To give someone a small gift
To ask someone a tricky question
To leave a place very quickly
questions.answer: To fail to show up for a meeting
questions.explanation: The idiom means standing someone up after arranging to meet them.

questions.prompt: In 'Il partit sans se retourner', which tense is the verb in?
questions.explanation: Partit is the third person form of the narrative tense used in written French.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ soient les difficultés, nous continuerons.' (whatever the difficulties may be)
questions.explanation: Quel que agrees with the noun that follows, and difficultés is feminine plural.

questions.prompt: In 'Je crains qu'il ne soit trop tard', what is the role of 'ne'?
options: It is expletive and carries no negative meaning
It is a formal replacement for pas
It marks the sentence as a question
questions.answer: It is expletive and carries no negative meaning
questions.explanation: After verbs of fearing, this ne is stylistic and the speaker does fear it is too late.
